content.problemStatement:
Given a sequence AAA of length NNN and an integer MMM, such that the elements of AAA are **pairwise distinct**.

For each kkk (1≤k≤M)(1\le k\le M)(1≤k≤M), find the number of subsequences Ai1,Ai2,…,AipA_{i_1},A_{i_2},\dots,A_{i_p}Ai1​​,Ai2​​,…,Aip​​ of AAA satisfying the following conditions:


Condition 111: ∑j=1pAij=k\sum_{j=1}^{p}A_{i_j}=k∑j=1p​Aij​​=k.
Condition 222: Let f(l,r)=max⁡i=lrAif(l,r)=\max_{i=l}^{r}A_if(l,r)=maxi=lr​Ai​. Then, for each jjj (1≤j≤p−1)(1\le j\le p-1)(1≤j≤p−1), f(ij,ij+1)>max⁡(Aij,Aij+1)f(i_j,i_{j+1})\gt\max(A_{i_j},A_{i_{j+1}})f(ij​,ij+1​)>max(Aij​​,Aij+1​​) holds.

If p=1p = 1p=1 (subsequence is of length 111), this condition is considered to be true.
Find the number of subsequences satisfying both the conditions for each kkk (1≤k≤M)(1\le k\le M)(1≤k≤M). Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line contains two integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\dots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print MMM numbers in a new line. The ithi^{th}ith number is the answer when k=ik=ik=i, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: All elements in AAA are pairwise distinct.
Sum of NNN across all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1032\cdot 10^32⋅103.
Sum of MMM across all test cases does not exceed 8⋅1038\cdot 10^38⋅103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111:


k=1k=1k=1: The only subsequence satisfying both the conditions is {1}\{1\}{1}.

The sum of all elements of this subsequence is 111.
Since there is only one element in the subsequence, we consider the second condition to be true.



k=2k=2k=2: The only subsequence satisfying both the conditions is {2}\{2\}{2}.

The sum of all elements of this subsequence is 222.
Since there is only one element in the subsequence, we consider the second condition to be true.



k=3k=3k=3: The subsequences satisfying both the conditions are {3}\{3\}{3} and {2,1}\{2,1\}{2,1}. For the subsequence {2,1}\{2,1\}{2,1}:

The sum of all elements of this subsequence is 2+1=32+1=32+1=3.
There are 222 elements in the subsequence. The indices of these elements in the sequence are 111 and 555 respectively. Here, f(1,5)=max⁡i=15Ai=5f(1,5) = \max_{i=1}^{5}A_i = 5f(1,5)=maxi=15​Ai​=5 and max⁡(2,1)=2\max(2, 1) = 2max(2,1)=2. Thus, f(1,5)>max⁡(A1,A5)f(1, 5) > \max(A_1, A_5)f(1,5)>max(A1​,A5​).



k=4k=4k=4: The subsequences satisfying both the conditions are {4}\{4\}{4} and {3,1}\{3,1\}{3,1}. For the subsequence {3,1}\{3,1\}{3,1}:

The sum of all elements of this subsequence is 3+1=43+1 = 43+1=4.
There are 222 elements in the subsequence. The indices of these elements in the sequence are 333 and 555 respectively. Here, f(3,5)=max⁡i=35Ai=5f(3,5) = \max_{i=3}^{5}A_i = 5f(3,5)=maxi=35​Ai​=5 and max⁡(3,1)=3\max(3, 1) = 3max(3,1)=3. Thus, f(3,5)>max⁡(A3,A5)f(3, 5) > \max(A_3, A_5)f(3,5)>max(A3​,A5​).



k=5k=5k=5: The subsequences satisfying both the conditions are {3}\{3\}{3}, {4,1}\{4,1\}{4,1} and {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3}. For the subsequence {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3}:

The sum of all elements of this subsequence is 2+3=52+3=52+3=5.
There are 222 elements in the subsequence. The indices of these elements in the sequence are 111 and 333 respectively. Here, f(1,3)=max⁡i=13Ai=4f(1,3) = \max_{i=1}^{3}A_i = 4f(1,3)=maxi=13​Ai​=4 and max⁡(2,3)=3\max(2, 3) = 3max(2,3)=3. Thus, f(1,3)>max⁡(A1,A3)f(1, 3) > \max(A_1, A_3)f(1,3)>max(A1​,A3​).



k=6k=6k=6: The only subsequence satisfying both the conditions is {2,3,1}\{2,3,1\}{2,3,1}.

The sum of all elements of this subsequence is 2+3+1=62+3+1=62+3+1=6.
There are 333 elements in the subsequence. The indices of these elements in the sequence are 1,31,31,3 and 555 respectively. Here, f(1,3)=max⁡i=13Ai=4f(1,3) = \max_{i=1}^{3}A_i = 4f(1,3)=maxi=13​Ai​=4 and max⁡(2,3)=3\max(2, 3) = 3max(2,3)=3. Thus, f(1,3)>max⁡(A1,A3)f(1, 3) > \max(A_1, A_3)f(1,3)>max(A1​,A3​). Similarly, f(3,5)=max⁡i=35Ai=5f(3,5) = \max_{i=3}^{5}A_i = 5f(3,5)=maxi=35​Ai​=5 and max⁡(3,1)=3\max(3, 1) = 3max(3,1)=3. Thus, f(3,5)>max⁡(A3,A5)f(3, 5) \gt \max(A_3, A_5)f(3,5)>max(A3​,A5​).
